name: Thread nonce & auth_time through the authorization code
outcome: A nonce supplied at /authorize, and a consumer-supplied auth_time, survive the authorize-to-token round trip for both JWT and opaque authorization codes.
scope: Add nonce and auth_time to the authorization request, auth code entity, and payload, propagate them through both encoders, and enforce the max_age rule. No ID token minting yet.
acceptance_criteria: A nonce supplied at /authorize survives to the resolved auth code payload for both JWT and opaque codes
Requesting max_age without supplying authTime throws an OAuthException that redirects with an error parameter, not an unhandled exception
The other OIDC authorization params are parsed onto AuthorizationRequest but not acted on
steps: AuthorizationRequest — add nonce and authTime fields plus a parsed bag of inert OIDC params (prompt, maxAge, loginHint, display, uiLocales, acrValues, idTokenHint)
OAuthAuthCode entity and PayloadAuthCode — add optional nonce and auth_time fields
AuthCodeGrant.validateAuthorizationRequest — read nonce and the OIDC params from the query string when OIDC is enabled

AuthCodeGrant.completeAuthorizationRequest — set nonce and authTime on the auth code entity, and throw when max_age was requested but authTime is absent; the throw must be an OAuthException so it surfaces as an error redirect (the consumer's login UI has already run by this point), not an unhandled exception
JwtAuthCodeEncoder and OpaqueAuthCodeEncoder — carry nonce and auth_time across the authorize-to-token round trip
test/unit and test/e2e — cover nonce survival for both encoders and the max_age enforcement throw
notes: Opaque codes rebuild the payload from the persisted entity, so nonce/auth_time must live on OAuthAuthCode. Consumers persisting opaque codes must store the two new optional fields — documented in issue 9.
